title: 第一部分 基础训练 TEST 3

sections.title: 阅读理解 A
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给的 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
#### **Some interesting facts about the Summer Olympics**

* London in England is the only city to have hosted the Games three times (1908, 1948 and 2012). Paris in France will become the next city to do so when it hosts the 2024 Summer Games.
* The USA has hosted the Games four times but in three different cities.
* At the Paris Games in 1900, there were more athletes (运动员) than spectators (观众).
* The oldest athlete ever at the Games is Japan's Hiroshi Hoketsu. 79-year-old Hiroshi competed at the Tokyo 2020 Olympics.
* In the first modern Olympic Games in Athens in 1896, there were no gold medals (金牌). Winners first got gold medals in the 1904 Olympics in St. Louis, America.
* The record for the longest name for an Olympic champion (冠军) is by Thai weightlifter (举重运动员) Prapawadee Jaroenrattanatarakoon.

questions.prompt: So far, how many times has Paris held the Summer Olympics?
questions.translation: 到目前为止，巴黎举办过几次夏季奥运会？
questions.explanation: 根据文中首段“Paris in France will become the next city to do so when it hosts the 2024 Summer Games.”（法国巴黎在举办2024年夏季奥运会时，将成为下一个举办过三次奥运会的城市）可知，目前巴黎还没举办2024年奥运会，因此到目前为止只举办过两次。答案为B。

questions.prompt: There were fewer spectators than athletes at the ________.
questions.translation: 在________，观众人数少于运动员人数。
questions.explanation: 根据文中第三点“At the Paris Games in 1900, there were more athletes (运动员) than spectators (观众).”（在1900年的巴黎奥运会上，运动员多于观众）可知，观众人数少于运动员。答案为B。

questions.prompt: What can we know about Hiroshi Hoketsu?
options: A. He has competed the most times at the Summer Olympics.
B. He has won the most gold medals at the Summer Olympics.
C. He is the first gold medal winner at the Summer Olympics.
D. He is the oldest athlete to compete at the Summer Olympics.
questions.translation: 关于博津宽，我们可以知道什么？
questions.explanation: 根据文中第四点“The oldest athlete ever at the Games is Japan's Hiroshi Hoketsu.”（奥运历史上最年长的运动员是日本的博津宽）可知，他是参加奥运会最年长的运动员。答案为D。

questions.prompt: Winners of the Summer Olympic Games began to get gold medals in ________.
questions.translation: 夏季奥运会的获胜者在________开始获得金牌。
questions.explanation: 根据文中第五点“Winners first got gold medals in the 1904 Olympics in St. Louis, America.”（获胜者首次获得金牌是在1904年美国圣路易斯奥运会上）可知，是在1904年。答案为A。

questions.prompt: Where does the Olympic champion with the longest name come from?
questions.translation: 名字最长的奥运冠军来自哪里？
questions.explanation: 根据文中最后一点“The record for the longest name for an Olympic champion (冠军) is by Thai weightlifter (举重运动员)...”可知，他是泰国的举重运动员，来自泰国。答案为D。

sections.title: 阅读理解 B
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给的 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
A student has saved £3,000 on travel costs by staying in people's homes as a pet sitter (宠物保姆) while traveling across Europe.

Soriah said she got the idea for the job after seeing a video of people using a special app. The app allows people to stay in homes around the world for free as long as they look after the owner's pet.

Soriah is from America. After finishing her first year of university in the UK, Soriah has enjoyed several European trips. She said all she had to spend is about £400 on each trip.

Soriah started using the app to help her travel around Europe and did her first job in 2022. She went to Stuttgart, Germany to look after two cats. And she flew to Bern, Switzerland, to look after another cat. "I could eat the food in the house and use the owner's bikes," she said of that stay. "It was great, and the cat was lovely."

Soriah later stayed at a house in Amsterdam, the Netherlands, with her mom to look after a dog. She also traveled to Dublin, Ireland to look after rabbits. She said she highly recommended (推荐) pet-sitting to people.

questions.prompt: Where did Soriah get the idea of being a pet sitter?
questions.translation: 索里亚从哪里得到当宠物保姆的想法？
questions.explanation: 根据第二段首句“Soriah said she got the idea for the job after seeing a video of people using a special app.”可知，她是从视频中得到的想法。答案为A。

questions.prompt: How much did Soriah pay for her each trip in Europe?
questions.translation: 索里亚在欧洲的每次旅行支付了多少费用？
questions.explanation: 根据第三段最后一句“She said all she had to spend is about £400 on each trip.”可知，每次旅行花费大约400英镑。答案为B。

questions.prompt:
What could Soriah do when she looked after the cat in Bern?
① She could eat the food in the owner's house.
② She could use the owner's cars.
③ She could use the owner's bikes.
④ She could sleep in the owner's house.
questions.translation: 索里亚在伯尔尼照顾猫时可以做什么？
questions.explanation: 根据第四段最后两句“I could eat the food in the house and use the owner's bikes... It was great...”以及宠物保姆需要寄宿在主人的家里（staying in people's homes）可知，①③④正确，没有提到使用汽车（②）。答案为C。

questions.prompt: Soriah helped feed ________ in Dublin.
questions.translation: 索里亚在都柏林帮忙喂养了________。
questions.explanation: 根据最后一段第二句“She also traveled to Dublin, Ireland to look after rabbits.”可知，她在都柏林照顾的是兔子。答案为D。

questions.prompt: From the passage, we can know that ________.
options: A. Soriah went to college in America
B. Soriah enjoyed her job as a pet sitter
C. Soriah made lots of money by looking after others' pets
D. Soriah went to Amsterdam alone to look after a dog
questions.translation: 从文章中，我们可以知道________。
questions.explanation: 根据最后一段最后一句“She said she highly recommended pet-sitting to people.”（她强烈推荐当宠物保姆）以及第四段中提到“It was great, and the cat was lovely”可知，她非常喜欢这份工作。A选项与“university in the UK”不符；C选项与“for free”不符；D选项与“with her mom”不符。答案为B。

sections.title: 阅读理解 C
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给的 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
Jose Condori is a Peruvian boy. When he was just seven years old, he started the Bartselana Student Bank. The bank is very unusual. It not only helps students save money, but also helps them save the earth.

Condori thought of the idea after seeing his classmates throw away many old toys every year. Condori thought this was a big waste. After talking with his teachers about it, he knew he could do something. So he started the bank.

Here is how the bank works. Students bring in plastic (塑料的) waste, and the bank sells it to a recycling company (回收公司). Then the company turns some of the waste into new products. Condori's bank makes money from this process (过程). Part of the money is put into the students' bank accounts (账户). The students can take the money out when they reach their savings goals (储蓄目标).

At first, the bank had only 20 members. Now, there are 2,000 members. And the number is still rising. Jose Condori is bringing the bank to more cities. He wants to help more people save money while saving the environment. Will you become a member of the bank when it comes to your city one day?

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT true about Jose Condori?
options: A. He is an excellent boy from Peru.
B. He started a bank at the age of seven.
C. He asked his teachers' opinions on his idea.
D. He helps students save money instead of saving the environment.
questions.translation: 关于何塞·孔多里，下列哪项是不正确的？
questions.explanation: 根据第一段最后一句“It not only helps students save money, but also helps them save the earth.”（它不仅帮助学生存钱，也帮助他们拯救地球/保护环境）可知，D选项说“帮助学生存钱而不是保护环境”是错误的。答案为D。

questions.prompt: Why did Jose Condori want to start the bank?
options: A. Because his teachers wanted him to do so.
B. Because he wanted to turn waste into new products.
C. Because he thought it was a waste to throw away many old toys.
D. Because he wanted to help his classmates make more pocket money.
questions.translation: 为什么何塞·孔多里想要开办这家银行？
questions.explanation: 根据第二段前两句“Condori thought of the idea after seeing his classmates throw away many old toys every year. Condori thought this was a big waste.”可知，他是因为觉得扔掉旧玩具太浪费而萌生了想法并创办了银行。答案为C。

questions.prompt:
What is the right order of how the bank works?
① Sell the waste to a recycling company.
② Get money from the recycling company.
③ Get some plastic waste from the students.
④ Put some money into students' bank accounts.
questions.translation: 该银行工作的正确顺序是什么？
questions.explanation: 根据第三段可知，学生先带入塑料垃圾（③），银行把垃圾卖给回收公司（①），银行从这个过程中赚钱（②），部分资金存入学生账户（④）。所以正确顺序为③①②④。答案为C。

questions.prompt: To take the money out of the bank, students should ________.
options: C. bring something new to the bank
D. sell the waste to the recycling company
questions.translation: 要把钱从银行里取出来，学生应该________。
questions.explanation: 根据第三段最后一句“The students can take the money out when they reach their savings goals (储蓄目标).”可知，要在达到储蓄目标时才能把钱取出来。答案为B。

questions.prompt: From the passage we can infer that ________.
options: A. the bank is part of the recycling company
B. the bank is becoming more and more popular
C. some teachers and Condori opened the bank together
D. students liked this unusual bank very much at the beginning
questions.translation: 从文章中我们可以推断出________。
questions.explanation: 根据最后一段“At first, the bank had only 20 members. Now, there are 2,000 members. And the number is still rising.”可知，会员人数从20人增加到2000人且仍在增长，说明银行越来越受欢迎。答案为B。

sections.title: 阅读理解 D
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给 of A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
Owls are known as "birds of the night". This means that they sleep in the day and feed at night.

Owls are birds that feed on small creatures (生物), like mice, bats and frogs. They have good eyesight and sharp hearing. They hardly miss their "food" in the dark. They can come at other small creatures without any noise with the help of their soft feathers (羽毛).

Owls have large heads and round faces. They have a pair of big round eyes that make them look awake at all times. But the eyes are so big that they can't move. Owls also have a smaller visual (视觉的) field than people do. An owl's visual field is about 110 degrees while the human's can reach about 180 degrees. For an owl to focus well, it must turn its head to get an object into its visual field.

The big eye size is very important for seeing at night. It helps an owl to see well in the dark. And an owl looks smart with big eyes. People can find many pictures of owls in storybooks. And they are also mentioned in the saying, "As wise as an owl." Don't you hope you are as wise as an owl?

questions.prompt: What is the main food of owls?
options: D. Creatures living in water like fish.
questions.translation: 什么是猫头鹰的主要食物？
questions.explanation: 根据第二段第一句“Owls are birds that feed on small creatures (生物), like mice, bats and frogs.”可知，是以小生物为食。答案为C。

questions.prompt: Owls can move quickly and quietly, thanks to ________.
options: D. their large heads and round faces
questions.translation: 猫头鹰能快速且安静地移动，幸亏了________。
questions.explanation: 根据第二段最后一句“They can come at other small creatures without any noise with the help of their soft feathers (羽毛).”可知，是得益于柔软的羽毛。答案为A。

questions.prompt: An owl has to turn its head to focus well because ________.
options: A. its head is too large
B. it has a smaller visual field
D. it looks awake at all times
questions.translation: 猫头鹰必须转头才能聚焦，是因为________。
questions.explanation: 根据第三段最后两句“Owls also have a smaller visual (视觉的) field than people do... For an owl to focus well, it must turn its head to get an object into its visual field.”可知，因为它的视野较小。答案为B。

questions.prompt: If people say you are "as wise as an owl", they mean ________.
options: B. you often stay up late
D. you can bring bad luck
questions.translation: 如果人们说你“像猫头鹰一样聪明”，他们的意思是________。
questions.explanation: “As wise as an owl” 意为像猫头鹰一样聪明/睿智（wise = clever）。答案为A。

questions.prompt: Which of the following is TRUE according to this passage?
options: A. Although an owl has big eyes, it can hardly see anything in the daytime.
B. Owls have a bigger visual field than people do.
C. It's difficult for owls to find their food because they need to turn their heads.
D. An owl hardly fails to catch a small creature in the dark.
questions.translation: 根据文章，下列哪项是正确的？
questions.explanation: 根据第二段第三句“They hardly miss their 'food' in the dark.”（它们在黑暗中几乎不会错过猎物/捕猎极少失手）可知，D选项“在黑暗中极少失手”是正确的。A选项“白天几乎看不见任何东西”文中未提及；B选项“视野比人更大”错误，应为更小；C选项“捕食很困难”与事实不符。答案为D。

sections.title: 阅读理解 E
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料，从每题所给的 A、B、C、D 四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
Nice photos show how a farmer used 250,000 sunflowers to create a fun trail (小径) in his field, bringing in visitors around the world.

Crawford Niven, 26, came up with the amazing idea of creating a sunflower trail three years ago. Crawford said he was not sure that sunflowers would grow well so far north in Scotland at first. Sunflowers need sunshine and there's not usually too much of it in northern Scotland. But the plants actually grew pretty well. And now thousands of visitors come to his farm to walk among the beautiful sunflowers, which they're even allowed to pick and take home.

"I started with 10 acres (英亩), but now we're up to about 13. It offers a photo chance and people love it," said Crawford. "The first time we opened the sunflower trail, lots of people came to have fun. The roads weren't nice and many people got caught in a traffic jam. But now it's under control," he added.

Visitors from all over the world are welcome to take either a short 20-minute walk, which has games for kids, or the longer 30-minute to one-hour walk that's more suited (适合的) to adults. But they need to book online before they come, with adult tickets costing £7.75 and kids below the age of 12 going free.

questions.prompt: How old was Crawford when he wanted to create a sunflower trail?
questions.translation: 克劳福德想创建向日葵步道时是多少岁？
questions.explanation: 根据第二段首句“Crawford Niven, 26, came up with the amazing idea of creating a sunflower trail three years ago.” 可知，他现在26岁，这个想法是三年前萌生的，所以当时是23岁（26-3=23）。因此答案为A。

questions.prompt: Why did Crawford worry that sunflowers wouldn't grow well in northern Scotland?
options: A. Because they need too much water.
B. Because it was too hot in northern Scotland.
C. Because there wasn't usually too much sunshine there.
D. Because the soil wasn't good enough for sunflowers.
questions.translation: 为什么克劳福德担心向日葵在苏格兰北部会长得不好？
questions.explanation: 根据第二段第三句“Sunflowers need sunshine and there's not usually too much of it in northern Scotland.”可知，因为向日葵需要阳光，而苏格兰北部通常阳光不足。因此答案为C。

questions.prompt: How many acres of sunflowers does Crawford have now?
questions.translation: 克劳福德现在拥有多少英亩的向日葵？
questions.explanation: 根据第三段首句“I started with 10 acres (英亩), but now we're up to about 13.”可知，现在大约有13英亩。因此答案为C。

questions.prompt: What problem did many visitors meet when the sunflower trail first opened?
options: A. They got lost on the farm.
B. They got caught in a traffic jam.
C. They couldn't find any sunflowers.
D. They were not allowed to take photos.
questions.translation: 向日葵步道第一次开放时，很多游客遇到了什么问题？
questions.explanation: 根据第三段第三句“The roads weren't nice and many people got caught in a traffic jam.”可知，道路状况不佳，许多游客遇到了堵车（交通堵塞）问题。因此答案为B。

questions.prompt: If Tom goes to the sunflower trail with his wife and 11-year-old son, how much should he pay?
questions.translation: 如果汤姆和妻子以及11岁的儿子去向日葵步道，他应该付多少钱？
questions.explanation: 根据最后一段最后一句“adult tickets costing £7.75 and kids below the age of 12 going free.”可知，成人票为7.75英镑，12岁以下儿童免费。汤姆和妻子共需付两张成人票：7.75 * 2 = 15.50英镑，11岁的儿子免费。因此一共需要付15.5英镑。答案为C。
